findings: E1 used the Skill name as if it were a fact source and omitted a falsification condition.
E2 did not prove facts, sources, and risks stayed unchanged.
E3 rejected unsourced recommendations but omitted activity, time, derivation, and not-applicable fields.
E4 correctly blocked when no scan target was supplied.
E1 still mixed confirmed facts, hypotheses, and the decision.
E2 claimed a no-drift check without an auditable baseline.
E3 found a catalog conflict but omitted the local catalog fingerprint and replay command.

attempts.normalization: Tracked artifacts add one final newline; content is otherwise byte-identical to the recovered temporary outputs.

results.evidence: Confirmed facts, hypotheses, decision, unknowns, minimum experiment, and falsification condition were explicitly separated.
The rewrite retained an identifiable input baseline and an auditable no-drift check for facts, numbers, sources, and risk.
The run challenged the claimed catalog miss, recorded four local matches, a replay command, observation time, catalog SHA-256, and unverified external boundaries.
The run blocked before scanning, did not choose a local target, recorded non-execution, and made no security claim.

boundary: This is a failure-first synthetic replay covering one representative output per evidence profile. It does not replace real domain inputs or user outcomes for all 26 Skills.
